bits.premise: The Amish lifestyle is a long-running scam designed to confuse modern society, evidenced by their media ubiquity and the scammer logic of claiming their cameras don't work.

lines.text: - Hello.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I noticed patterns for a living,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And I noticed something, the Amish.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They used to be elusive, but now they're everywhere.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: TLC, HBO, I don't even have cable, but I see them every day.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'm starting to think that maybe there's a scam going on here.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I'm not sure.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I'm at that age now where everything, I think, is a scam.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: But they pretend they can't have cell phones,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: But then, but then that's scamming 101.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: No, my camera doesn't work.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: But anyway, so I feel like they had a meeting sometime.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They had, they said,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: let's, there was no electricity candles.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Let's build barns, churn butter and
lines.label: setup

lines.text: confuse the hell out of the society.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: That's what it's like basically.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: And I know one person at a meeting said,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: This could have been an email.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: But anyway, I'm just saying, check the barns for wifi.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: This has really changed.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Anyway, I tried.
lines.label: fluff
